spell.spell_desc: To keep the enemy at bay and also to kill him from a distance

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tanks who Set like Tigreal are great very Chang'e and hitting enemies with her ultimate.
Hylos and other tanks who can zone and protect Chang'e are great for giving her vision and keeping her shield up. Hylos also slows down enemies which is perfect for Chang'e to easily hunt them down.

Angela actually works quite well with Chang'e as she keeps her shield alive. Chang'e is fragile, so Angela can keep her staying aggressive enough to force the enemies back. 

Lolita also works well with her by also keeping her shield alive.

Terizla acts as great Frontline wall for Chang'e. They both work well in melting enemies and with Terizla's ultimate, Chang'e can easily land hers on the enemies. EXP heroes like this pair well with her.

counters.counter_desc: Esmeralda easily feeds on the shield Chang'e needs. She can steal it leaving Chang'e weak and defenseless. Not too mention, but she can easily dive her.
Lylia is great at poking, easily attacking Chang'e and popping her shield. Lylia's ultimate also helps her reposition to avoid the ultimate of Chang'e.
Gusion and other dive assasins can easily punish her when she tries being aggressive. They can even get to her while she's hiding in the backline.

Hylos and other tanky heroes can "tank" her damage. They can protect their allies and soak up all the damage from her ultimate. However, her continuous damage (and her short Ult CD) will eventually shred their health bars.
Belerick can also soak up her damage, but he can reflect it back to her effortlessly breaking her shield.
Kagura out-pokes and out-ranges Chang'e. Kagura's umbrella can easily remove her shield and avoid her ultimate.
Lolita's 2nd Skill clearly deflects her ultimate and sends it right back to her, making her ultimate useless if not dangerous to use in teamfights.
Pharsa who relies on keeping her distance and positioning is threatened by Chang'e and her long range ultimate. Pharsa's ultimate keeps her stuck in one place vulnerable to Chang'e Ult.
Much like Pharsa, Ixia's Ultimate keeps her in place making her vulnerable to Chang'e Ult. But she also lacks mobility in general, making it easy for Chang'e to hunt her down.

Believe it or not, but Yu Zhong gets shredded by Chang'e and her Ultimate. He needs to close the gap in order to kill her (something he is good at), but Chang'e can actually keep her distance and kill him before he can reach her in time. 

I've used Chang'e to face Yu Zhong many times and won almost all the time.

dominants.dominant_desc: Early one Chang'e already does great damage. Select the 2nd Skill first and try to keep your shield up. After you get your first skill, focus on clearing your minions still and poke the enemy mid laner as much as possible. Chang'e has fast wave clearing, so she can quickly rotate and help another lane. Prioritize getting her ultimate as soon as possible in order to harrass enemies like the enemy jungler taking his buffs. Her ultimate is also very useful in clearing minion waves and pushing the enemy mid laner back into their turret.

Here, your main goal is to be everywhere. Use your ultimate to clear you minion waves and start rotating to allies who need your help in poking enemies. Join teamfights and focus on zoning enemies out (like with her ultimate). She may even have enough damage to take out squishy or low health enemies. You should be as aggressive (and as annoying) as possible in order to pressure and starve the enemies. Make use of her pushing capabilities in order to quickly take down turrets.

Everyone deals massive damage during this stage but so do you. Your goal is to stay alive, so be a bit less aggressive and be more aware. Chang'e can still pressure enemies and zone them out during this stage. Her Ultimate cooldown is basically spammable now, so stay a safe distance away from them. Stay behind your tank/frontliner especially when you can't see enemies. If there are still turrets left standing, prioritize taking them down. If possible, try doing a sneaky backdoor or at least focus on clearing minions to continue pressuring the enemies.

- use Chang'e when you have a tanky frontliner. 
- keep her Crescent Moon Shield alive. 
- be aggressive, oppress enemies. 
- rotate, be everywhere, help allies. 
- annoy enemies, steal buffs. 
- focus on pushing turrets and clearing minion waves. 
- be careful in the late game; use ult more

emblems.emblem_desc:
Mage Emblem would fit her by default, but the Assasins Emblem could also work. 

Rupture would give her adaptive penetration that pairs great with her skills. (Especially her Ultimate). 

The 2nd emblem is more optional but Bargain Hunter is what I'd recommend so that you can get her items faster. 

Impure Rage would again give her extra adaptive damage and do skill damage based on 4% of the target's Max HP. Her ultimate would most definitely benefit from this.
This emblem is for when you want to prioritize attack speed or even want to use Chang'e as a Gold Laner.

equips.equip_desc:
Magic Boots offer great CD reduction, but Arcane Boots can also work with its magic penetration. 

Enchanted Talisman mainly helps with the mana problem. Chang'e uses up a lot of mana from her skills which all have relatively short cooldowns, so she'll be draining up often. 

Glowing Wand is great for melting enemies and of course giving the helpful Lifebane effect. This effect also helps out in teamfights as enemies who rely on lifesteal can do nothing as Chang'e can apply it to them from a long distance away. 

Divine Glaive, Lightning Truncheon, and Holy Crystal add more penetration and magic damage making Chang'e even more threatening. 

Extra: 
Sky Peircer is optional but also great with Chang'e. (I use it almost all the time). Usually should be your 2nd-3rd item, and replacing Divine Glaive. 

Blood Wings helps protect with Crescent Moon shield and adds a bit of additional protection. 

Ice Queen Wand is also quite popular to use with her.

This build is for when you want to prioritize attack speed for Chang'e. Chang'e actually work really well with this and this boost her pushing capabilities (which is already great). 

Swift Boots, Feather of Heaven, and Golden Staff boost her attack speed. While Holy Crystal, Blood Wings, and Divine Glaive help her sustain her magic damage. 

And yes Sky Peircer is an optional spare option. (2nd-3rd item)

Sky Peircer actually works really well with her as it makes her and her ultimate even more oppresssive. She can use her ultimate to finish off enemies trying to run away and Sky Peircer would help secure that. 

Blood Wings would help protect her Crescent Moon Shield.

hero.hero_overview: Chang'e is a very powerful artillery mage, with long range and relentless sustained damage. Unlike other mages who rely on combos or crowd control, Chang'e delivers pressure through her rapid firing Ultimate with short cooldown, and her fast enhanced basic attacks.
hero.hero_strength: Chang'e possesses a specialized damage modifier against non-hero units, allowing her to vaporize minion waves quickly and rotate to other lanes faster than almost any other mid-laner. Her high base movement speed and shield-based buffs allow her to kite enemies effortlessly, dealing massive damage while remaining just out of reach of their abilities. This also makes her a nightmare for enemy Junglers, as she can safely "snipe" the Blue or Red buffs and steal them very quickly.
hero.hero_weakness: Her skills and enhanced basic attacks are dependent on her shield (Crescent Moon) and they become much weaker without it. If an enemy manages to break her shield, her damage output is effectively halved, and she becomes less threatening. Because she lacks a "hard" blink or dash, she is highly susceptible to high-mobility assassins and "dive" compositions that can close the gap quickly.

data.recommend: Chang'e is great for Ranking Up even in Solo Que. She is very beginner-friendly, but still very strong in game. She may even be used for a Back door strategy if you've become desperate.

spell.spell_desc: Chang'e lacks a "blink" or dash skill making her vulnerable to enemies who can dive her or at least close the gap. Without her shield, Chang'e moves much slower and is much weaker to do anything. Flicker would provide her this escape in case of emergencies.

data.title: Chang'e Guide

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Atlas work very well with chang e because he can pull multiple enemies together using his ultimate, allowing chang e to deal full damage with her ultimate at once. He also protects her as a frontline tank, giving her safe positioning during team fights. However, if atlas missses his ultimate the combk become less effective

counters.counter_desc: Gusion counters chang e because his high burst damage can eliminate her quickly before she casts her ultimate. His mobility lets him dodge her skills and engage safely, making chang e poke and zoning mispositions, chang e can poke him down from range but overall gusion dominates the matchup

dominants.dominant_desc: Chang e is very strong in the mid to late game due to his burst damage and long ultimate range. When laning, always clear the minion wave with skill 1 and don't overextendbwithout vision because chang e is easy to kill assassins. Use skill 2 to shiled before a team fight or want to poke an enemy. In team fights, stand behind tanks and use ultimates for zoning, breaking enemy formations as well as stealing objectives such as turtles or lords from a distance

dominants.dominant_desc: She needs to be careful in the early game, always clearing waves quickly and rotating to apply pressure and support her team. However, her main focus early on should be farming to scale into the mid and late game.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Inspire emblem ensures she has good mana regeneration, as well as reduced cooldown on her skills.

equips.equip_desc: With this equipment, she will have strong cooldown reduction, allowing her to use her skills and Ultimate constantly. She can clear a wave and quickly rotate to the next lane to clear again with her Ultimate. Golden Staff is essential for focusing on turrets.

hero.hero_overview: Change is a dps mage with extremely fast wave clear and strong long-range poke. Her Ultimate can be used roughly every 10 seconds, allowing constant pressure on enemies, objectives, and turrets. She excels at safe sieging without exposing herself.
hero.hero_strength: Change clears waves instantly and rotates quickly. Her short-cooldown Ultimate provides constant poke in fights and objective control. She can damage turrets and dominates mid to late game when protected by her team.
hero.hero_weakness: Change is fragile and vulnerable to assassins and high-mobility heroes. She lacks strong escape tools and relies heavily on positioning. If enemies dive or catch her off guard, she can be eliminated very quickly.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Khufra is the dream of every mage hero. Can prepare you safe positions to cast your ultimate. He won't let assassins to approach you.
His ult and second skills applies Slowing effects, which just complement Chang'e 's. In addition, Hylos can tank great amont of damage, which is good news for Chang'e.
Similar to Khufra.
Silvanna's ultimate can be beneficial for Chang'e.

Their skillsets are inconpatible. Grock is better with
ambush-burst type mages, such as Kagura and Selena.
If Angela is picked, there will be no tanks to protect and create positions for Chang'e. Angela is the example here, but Chang'e is incompatible with every healing support. Chang'e needs a tank.
Fredrinn Jungler and Chang'e is a serious threat. Fredrinn is a tank/fighter, who can act as a Shield for Chang'e. He also has lots of annoying CC skills.

counters.counter_desc:
Lolita is the BIGGEST and BIGGEST counter of Chang'e. If you pick Chang'e , and then the opponent picks Lolita, say goodbye to your ranked mode star.

Lolita's 2nd skill activated a Shield which reflects every projectile skill, and so Chang'e 's Meteor Rain ultimate. Never pick Chang'e before the opponent picks a tank hero except Lolita.

Barats gets really annoying once he gets overfed. He almost becomes immune to damage. He can absorb your ult and nothing will happen to him.

But if you are the one who got overfed, tables will surely turn.
Atlas is very dangerous in late game. He can reach to Chang'e even if she is staying behind the tower.
Ling can easily catch her by jumping and travelling through walls easily.
Fanny can easily catch and burst down Chang'e.
Hayabusa can also hunt Chang'e easily.
Kagura is a dangerous mage who can set up ambushes for Chang'e. But Chang'e can outperform her in teamfights .
Once Helcurt reaches to Chang'e , there will be no escape.
They both counter each other. Esmeralda is a tank/mage who steals her opponents' Shield. However, Chang'e is a tank slayer, and Esmeralda is a tank too.
He will force Chang'e to use her ult every time in 1vs1. Chang'e 's ult will be just wasted. Hanzo can easily shut Chang'e down.
Jhonson can also crash into Chang'e in his car form out of a sudden and cause her to die.
Cici can keep up with Chang'e 's speed and Dodge her Meteor Rain.
Benedetta can catch her and dodge her skills .
Same with Benedetta.
Harley can reach out to Chang'e and burst her down.
Despte the fact that Lukas is one of the most broken heroes to be introduced to MlBB universe, Chang'e can melt hım. Especially the Tiktok build will hit hım hard LoL.
Except for his ult, Alpha has no other mobility. Once he uses his ult , he is an open target for Chang'e.
She can also get melted.
Chang'e 's ult range may overwhelm Aurora.
They are almost equal.
Depends on who gets more gold.
Real pain for Balmond.
Hard times for Alucard.
Probably depends on the player. But Zhask is better in the current meta.
Chang'e can outperform her in solo rank. However, in 5vs5 Esports  games , nobody cares about Chang'e while Yve is an autoban hero.
Mathilda can easily get close to Chang'e.
Chang'e can outperform him.
Real pain part three.
Chang'e can challenge hım.
Lancelot can fastly catch Chang'e and get away with it.
Real pain part four.
You can safely targetlock Miya in teamfights with your Meteor Rain.
Chang'e can buy anti-heal item(Glowing Wand) and greatly challenge Estes.
A dangerous assassin for Chang'e.
Qiuck! Run! Roger is coming! (Chang'e hero voicelines)

dominants.dominant_desc:
Let me tell you about her skills. Her skills and basic attack has two modes: Basic and enhanced. Each time you use your 2nd skill , it will give you a Shield. As long as the Shield is active,  your skills and basic attacks will be enhanced. If you take damage and lose the Shield, your skills and basic attacks go  back to their basic forms.


Skill 1 :

Basic: Sends a magic orb to a designated direction. Deals damage and slow downs the opponent's.

Enhanced: Number of magic orbs are Increased. Deals higher damage and better Slowing effect.


Skill 2 :

This skill has no two modes. It is the skill that gives you the shield when you use it. It also gives you movement speed bonus for a short duration.


Skill 3 : 

Basic : Sends a Meteor Rain to a designated direction.

Enhanced : Width of the ultimate is increased.


Basic attack: 

Basic:Only a regular basic attack that only deals physical damage. Each time, you can shoot 1 basic attack.

Enhanced: You can shoot 3 basic attacks at the same time. And they can deal Hybrid damage(=both magical and physical damage).

Note:Her enhanced basic attacks reduces the second skills cooldown by 1seconds for each hit.



Passive Skill:

Each time she deals damage to an opponent hero, a mark appears beneath them, which can be stacked up to 20 times.

Each stack applies them 1% Slowing effect.

Focus on clearing the Minion waves and opening your ult.

Be careful not to be ganked by tanks. Some aggressive tanks May attack you before they reach lvl4 and open their ultimate skills, such as Grock and Khufra.

Watch out for enemy jungler. They will , for the most of the time , will gang your marksman first. But, who Knows? Maybe , you will be the first Target.

You can try an early gang with your tank against the enemy marksman if your tank is trustable.

Turtle is important since it gives each ally hero exp and gold.

Always go to turtle fight.

Stay behind not to get caught.

Always check your tank if he/she is going to try an outplay with flicker, which will give you a great opportunity to cast ultimate.


You May also want to ult the turtle to help your jungler kill it as fast as possible.

Mainly follow your tank. Watch out for ambushes by the opponent mage and assassins. Don't let opponent's fighter play safely with your annoying skillset and poke.


If you are overfed, use your ult aggressively . Even if it won't kill Anyone at all.


You can steal the opponent's blue buff with your ult too.

Stay behind the tower and your tank. Always follow your tank.  Use your ult at the rightest moment.

If the opponent team is coming for a big push with lord, save your ult for the lord .

You can also buy items such as Winter Crown and Immortality.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Use Mage emblem for early game damage.
Use Rupture for extra penetration.
Use Weapons Master to maximize Holy Cristal's damage.
Use Impure Rage to poke tanks and for Mana regen.

This is the same emblem set with the 1st, but it costs less.
Replace Weapons Master with Bargain Hunter to purchase items with a 5% discount.

Don't buy Holy Cristal to maximize Bargain Hunter's potential.

This is the emblem set for the Tiktok build. U can replace Weakness Finder with War Cry.

Weakness Finder will help you reduce the opponent's attack speed each time you deal damage with your basic attacks. 

The Tiktok build aims to utilize Chang'e 's basic attacks rather than her skillset damage. So Weakness Finder is perfect for this.

U may want to use this if the opponent team has basic attack based fighters, such as Argus or Zilong.


On the other hand , War Cry wil let you deal +8% damage after you deal damage for 3 consecutive times. Since the Tiktok build focuses on attack speed, it will easily trigger War Cry.

Replace Rupture with Inspire for a cooldown reduction build. 

This emblem and build combination will focus on using a lot of ultimate skill, creating an unbreakable defence.
Use this alternative for maximum magic penetration.

equips.equip_desc:
Use Arcane boots for magic penetration(+10).

Use Glowing Wand for:

1)Anti-heal effect: Glowing Wand reduces HP regen and Shield gain by 50%, which is a Real trouble for many fighters and tanks.

2)Poking : Glowing Wand's passive effect will deal 3% of the maximum HP of the opponent's who gets hit, a real trouble for tanks again. If you combine this with the emblem talent "Impure Rage" , it will be 7%. 

3)Extra HP:
+300 HP coming from it will boost your survivability. 

Use Ice Queen's Wand for:

1)Slowing effects: This item can slow your opponents up to 30%, and when combined with Chang'e 's passive, up to 50%. This is really a great tool against fighters and tanks.

2)HP bonus and Lifesteal:
This item also gives +300HP and +10% magic lifesteal , which will help Chang'e to survive.

Use Holy Cristal for:
Maximize your magic damage.


Use Divine Glaive for:

Maximum magic penetration. Its passive ignores at least 40% of the opponents' magic defence.

Use Blood Wings for:

Burst Damage and extra Shield.

Blood Wings will grant you an extra shield corresponding your Magic power.
This build will cost less and its main focus will be to finish the game ass soon as possible.

This build seems like a complete troll but it is actually not. 

Despte the fact that Chang'e is a mage, this build lets her to melt her opponents in seconds with super-fast basic attacks, just like a marksman.

I don't play marksman too often, so I am not really able to explain this properly in detail, but for some reason, her attack speed goes crazy when Feather of Heaven and Golden Staff is combined.

Some brief explanation:
Swift boots, Feather of Heaven and Golden Staff boosts her attack speed. 

Holy Cristal boosts her magic damage.

Blood Wings enhances survival skills.

Divine Glaive provides magic penetration

This build mainly aims to use your ult skill more often, which is great for defending.

Little explanation: Genius Wand is for early game magic penetration.

Magic boots + Enchanted Talisman + Wishing Lantern + Mage emblem + Inspire will give you +45% cooldown reduction, you can cast your ultimate almost every 10 seconds in late game.

However, don't forget that this build may lack magic burst.

As a solution, once you ensure you have +45% cooldown reduction, you can buy items like Holy Cristal or Glowing Wand.

This build aims to break the oppoents magic defence.
You can use that kind of a build against extremely tanky opponents.

This build is merciless against tanks.

Glowing Wand deals damage based on Max HP.
Wishing Lantern deals damage based on current HP.
Holy Cristal boosts magic burst.
The remaining trio of items destroys magic defence.
Goodbye to tanks.

After completing your build, sell Genius Wand and buy Ice Queen's Wand instead.
You can also sell your boots and buy Enchanted Talisman for cooldown reduction.
equips.equip_title: Build 5 : Maximum Magic Penetration (Literal Tank Destroyer)

equips.equip_desc:
You can also try this 6 damage item build for late game. Except for Ice Queen Wand, every other item primarily provides Magic damage.

Almost no hero can stand this damage.

hero.hero_overview: She is a mid-laner mage. 4 to 5 years ago, she was one of the strongest heroes in the game, however, in the current meta, she is a situational pick.
hero.hero_strength: Her biggest strength is her pushing and defending capability thanks to her ultimate. Her ultimate sends meteor rain to a particular direction for a few seconds, which easily wipes all the Minion waves , and even the Lord. And as you can all predict, it can melt opponent heroes in seconds.
hero.hero_weakness:
However, her strong sides were utilized easily in the old meta. 

In todays meta,

1)Burst mages can outshine her in the early stages of the game.
2)She is an open target for assassins.
3)She does not have a CC skill.
4)The item "Radiant Armor" can left her shocked.
5)Some tanks, especially Lolita, can easily absorb her ulti's damage.

data.recommend: A hero that Anyone can play : Chang'e

spell.spell_desc:
There are various options for battle spell. I mean, really!

1)Flicker : This is the safest spell. Either to chase or to escape.

2)Sprint: This spell will boost your movement speed for a short duration. You can Chase your enemies doing the Sprint+ Ultimate skill combo (both gives movement speed). 
You can also use it to escape.

If there are tough fighters lacking CC skills, this may be a good choice.


3)Flameshot: If you are sure that opponent enemies are not a threat to you, you can pick spell to complete your damage combo.


4)Purify: Use this spell if the opponent team has strong CC.


5)Inspire: This is homestly a bit trolling but idk.

data.title: The Hero that Anyone Can Play : Chang'e

cooperates.cooperate_desc: When teaming with roamer badang which is now meta it was easy to kill enemy when badang use his second and first skill to stun enemy then ultimate them while Chang'e can use her ultimate to help dealing damage to the enemy and kill them faster.

counters.counter_desc: When one by one with Arlott it was hard to fight him because of his stun and penetration which can make Chang'e lost her second skill and become easy kill but when you with your team you can kill him when he focus on you team while you can focus dealing damage to him and kill him.

dominants.dominant_desc: In team fight Chang'e help team by poking the enemy and help push turrets due to her second skill and when to rotation,rotate to lane where really need help like mm were ambush by enemy so rotate there first after that help jungler kill turtle/lord if it's time because mage is like  the second roamer so it necessary for midlaner to rotate same as roamer.If there's a chance you can steal enemy buff when enemy jungler were busy war to increase team gold .

hero.hero_overview: Chang'e is a midliner hero with a Crescent Moon skill that gives her a shield and enhances her basic attacks. Her ultimate skill is Meteor Shower, which continuously fires meteors in a target direction.
hero.hero_strength: Chang'e is a strong character with high magical power and the ability to slow enemies. She can deal a lot of damage and has multiple skills that can be used in combination.
hero.hero_weakness: Chang'e's weakness is to tank heroes, who are the most durable heroes in the game.

data.title: Road to Pro

cooperates.cooperate_desc: With duos, I invite tank players like Tigreal and other CC tanks to burst my basic attack and ultimate. Chang e also push turrets very quickly in the mid to late game, with the full build, a turret will be destroyed in seconds and the base will be melted within 8 seconds if you constantly use your basic attacks in it in the late game.
Mathilda can help chang e rotate to gank lanes faster in the mid-late game once Chang e builds her core items.

counters.counter_desc: Loita can reflect your basic attacks, skill one and ultimate, making you useless as long as loita got her second skill, loita skill 1 and ultimate can also stun you, hence avoiding loita in matches or one she used her skill two is the best option to stay alive

dominants.dominant_desc: Upgrade skill 2 and launch 5 basic attacks to the creep the jungler is attacking to help him clear it faster, then clear the wave as fast as you can and see if any lanes need help, rotate to that lane. Make sure that you do not stay too long, in order to get your second minion wave. Get lvl 4 before turtle spawns and proceed to help your jungles attack the turtle, whenever you cleared your lane, make a point to tank other lanes but keep an eye in your turret and make sure it is not being pushed by enemies

emblems.emblem_desc: Infinite slowness with weakness finder since attack speed build is applied, bargain hunter for getting core items easier and swift for extra atk speed to get an early game advantage!

equips.equip_desc: Swift boots for movement speed for banking lanes. Build the core items, feather of heaven and golden staff for attack speed. Wishing lantern can counter high go heroes, with Chang e swift basic attacks, you can melt enemies along with a shield from blood wings to boost your survival, and finish it with holy crystal for the most magic power

hero.hero_strength: Since Chang E is strong in he early game, try poking enemies with your enhanced first skill, in mid game, after you get your core ark speed items, go a little aggressive with tank.
hero.hero_weakness: Avoid Lolita that can counter your basic attacks and ultimate, stay away from burst assassins like Nolan, Fanny and Ling since Chang E lacks mobility.

spell.spell_desc: Since Chang E lacks mobility, and only her second skill gives movement speed, flicker can get you good positioning for your ultimate or get you closer total the enemy to finish them with basic attacks. When seeing ling, fanny or Nolan, any burst assassin, flicker can save you

dominants.dominant_desc: Early game Try to help your jungler then go back to Lane and look for a level up to level 4 then if there is an opportunity take the enemy's blue Buff to slow down the level increase If you have got a skypepper, try to find a kill so that you won't have any trouble in the late game.

equips.equip_desc: Genius Wand is really needed at the beginning to be able to penetrate the opponent's defense, then skypiecer to get kills, holy crystal to add more painful damage, lightning truncheon To increase damage, if you want to slow down, replace it with Ice Queen, Blood Wings to make the change into lots of blood with the Shield.

spell.spell_desc: If enemy full crowd control use Purify

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Can initiate team and absorb damage, allowing Chang'e to deal damage from the backline.
Can quicky eliminate enemies after Chang'e softens them up with her skills
Provides mobility and healing, enhancing Chang'e's survivability in team fights
Can initiate fights and group together, allowing Chang'e to unleash her ultimate effectively.

counters.counter_desc: Can quickly burst her down before she can react.
His mobility allows him to dodge her skills and deal significant damage.
Can outmaneuver her and use her skills to control fights.
Can absord her damage and retaliate effectively.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Start with Skill 2 to clear minions faster and enhances your basic attacks. Focus in leveling up your Ultimate as it is your main source of damage.

Aim to reach level 4 quickly since your Ultimate is crucial for damage. Use your Ultimate to poke enemies and secure buffs when the enemy jungler is missing.

Always stay at the backline during team fights. This keeps you safe while you deal damage from a distance. Be cautious of assassins and crowd control heroes, as they can easily catch you if you are out of position.

Start with Skill 2 for shield and movement speed.
Follow up with Skill 1 to slow enemies and then unleash you Ultimate for maximum damage.

Your Ultimate deals extra damage to minions and monsters, making you effective in securing objectives like Turtle and Lord.
As much as possible try to steal also enemy buffs as this will hinder faster leveling of your enemy jungler.

emblems.emblem_desc: Rupture increases your magic penetration, making your skills hit harder. Weapon Master grants additional magic power from items, emblems, talents and skills. Impure rage provides extra damage whenever you hut an enemy with your skills, enhancing your burst potential.

equips.equip_desc:
Early Game: Arcane boots and Genius wand. Arcane boots provides movement speed and magic power, essential for mobility and damage. Genius wand increases your magic power and provides magic penetration. 
Core items: Glowing wand, ice queen wand and Blood wings
Glowing wand is one of the core items I should say to use for chang'e in combination with glowing wand. With this combination, it deals burn damage over time plus it slows the enemies hit by your skill, providing a better crowd control. Blood wings provides a large amount of magic power and shield based on your max HP, increasing your survivability in the game.

hero.hero_overview: Mid lane: sustained poke with good burst damage.
hero.hero_strength: Chang'e is another hero you will want to use in your journey to Mythic. With the correct positioning, she can deal a geat damage from afar by using her Meteor Shower + Starmoon shockwave combo.
hero.hero_weakness: Chang'e has some notable weakness that should be aware of: low defense, weak against assassins, lack of utility and reliance of positioning.

data.recommend: Feel free to share your thoughts about my guide. Thank you and have a nice day!

spell.spell_desc: Great for poking enemies from a distance and finishing off low-health targets. It provides extra damage.
